accordance with the targeted incentive compensation based on a percentage of the growth of Business Services revenue less its cost of goods sold and its direct expenses compared to the prior year.  The Compensation Committee believed that such a payment was appropriate given the strong performance of the Company's Business Services department.  The incentive plan for Mr. Pearce for 2014 will be the same as his 2013 incentive plan.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;text-decoration:underline;">Stock Option Plan</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">.</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#160;&#160;Options and awards, if granted to the Named Executive Officers, were granted pursuant to terms of our Stock Option Plan.&#160;&#160;In particular, the exercise price for options in each instance was the closing price for our Class A common stock on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on the day of the grant of the option.&#160;&#160;Options or awards, if granted, were granted contemporaneously with the approval of the Compensation Committee, typically early in the year in question or late in the previous year as described above.&#160;&#160;See "Part III &#8211; Item 11 &#8211; Compensation Discussion and Analysis:&#160;&#160;Elements of Compensation &#8211; Incentive Compensation Plan."</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">With limited exceptions (one involving Mr. Duncan, a Named Executive Officer), since mid-2009 we have used restricted stock in place of options.&#160;&#160;That is, on February&#160;8, 2010, we granted an option for 150,000 shares of Class A common stock to Mr. Duncan.&#160;&#160;That option vested on February 8, 2012.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">We adopted our stock option plan in 1986.&#160;&#160;It has been subsequently amended from time to time and presently is our Stock Option Plan, i.e., our Amended and Restated 1986 Stock Option Plan.&#160;&#160;Under our Stock Option Plan, we are authorized to grant awards and options to purchase shares of Class A common stock to selected officers, directors and other employees of, and consultants or advisors to, the Company and its subsidiaries.&#160;&#160;The options </font></div><br><div style="text-align:center;"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">60</font></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"><div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:8pt;"><a style="font-family:Arial;font-size:8pt;" href="#sBA5EB157D68474C91E2F82221DA9B816"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:8pt;">Table of Contents</font></a></div></div><br><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">are more specifically referred to as nonstatutory stock options or incentive stock options within Section 422 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended ("Internal Revenue Code").&#160;&#160;In addition, under the Stock Option Plan restricted stock awards may be granted as further described below.&#160;&#160;The selection of grantees for options and awards under the plan is made by our Compensation Committee.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">The number of shares of Class A common stock allocated to the Stock Option Plan is 15.7 million shares.&#160;&#160;The number of shares for which options or awards may be granted is subject to adjustment upon the occurrence of stock dividends, stock splits, mergers, consolidations and certain other changes in corporate structure or capitalization.&#160;&#160;As of December 31, 2013, there were 0.6 million shares subject to outstanding options under the Stock Option Plan, 1.2 million shares granted subject to vesting, 3.4 million share grants had vested, 8.6 million shares had been issued upon the exercise of options under the plan, 1.3 million shares repurchased by the plan and 3.2 million shares remained available for additional grants under the plan.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Restricted stock awards granted under the Stock Option Plan may be subject to vesting conditions based upon service or performance criteria as the Compensation Committee may specify.&#160;&#160;These specifications may include attainment of one or more performance targets.&#160;&#160;Shares acquired pursuant to such an award may not be transferred by the participant until vested.&#160;&#160;Unless otherwise provided by the Compensation Committee, a participant will forfeit any shares of restricted stock where the restrictions have not lapsed prior to the participant's termination of service with us.&#160;&#160;Participants holding restricted stock will have the right to vote the shares and to receive dividends paid, if any.&#160;&#160;However, those dividends or other distributions paid in shares will be subject to the same restrictions as the original award.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Our Compensation Committee selects each grantee and the time of grant of an option or award and determines the terms of each grant, including the number of shares covered by each grant and the exercise price.&#160;&#160;In selecting a participant, as well as in determining these other terms and conditions of each grant, our Compensation Committee takes into consideration such factors as it deems, in its sole discretion, relevant in connection with accomplishing the purpose of the plan.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Under our Stock Option Plan, our authority to modify or amend the plan is subject to prior approval of our shareholders only in cases of increasing the number of shares of our stock allocated to, and available and reserved for, issuance under the plan, changing the class of persons eligible to receive incentive stock options or where shareholder approval is required under applicable law, regulation or rule.&#160;&#160;One such law requiring shareholder approval before the Company may rely on it is Section 162(m) of the Internal Revenue Code.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Subject to these limitations, the Company may terminate or amend the Stock Option Plan at any time.&#160;&#160;However, no termination or amendment may affect any outstanding option or award unless expressly provided by the Compensation Committee.&#160;&#160;In any event, no termination or amendment of the plan may adversely affect an outstanding option or award without the consent of the participant unless necessary to comply with applicable law, regulation or rule.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">With limited exception, no maximum or minimum exists with regard to the amount, either in dollars or in numbers, of options that may be exercised in any year, either by a single optionee or by all optionees under our Stock Option Plan.&#160;&#160;At the 2002 annual meeting, our shareholders approved an amendment to the plan placing a limitation on accumulated grants of options of not more than 500,000 shares of Class A common stock per optionee per year.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">With these exceptions, there are no fixed limitations on the number or amount of securities being offered, other than the practical limitations imposed by the number of employees eligible to participate in the plan and the total number of shares of stock authorized and available for granting under the plan.&#160;&#160;&#160;Shares covered by options which have terminated or expired for any reason prior to their exercise are available for grant of new options pursuant to the plan.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><br><div style="text-align:center;"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">61</font></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"><div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:8pt;"><a style="font-family:Arial;font-size:8pt;" href="#sBA5EB157D68474C91E2F82221DA9B816"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:8pt;">Table of Contents</font></a></div></div><br><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;text-decoration:underline;">Perquisites</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">.</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#160;&#160;The Company provides certain perquisites to its Named Executive Officers.&#160;&#160;The Compensation Committee believes these perquisites are reasonable and appropriate and consistent with our awareness of perquisites offered by similar publicly traded companies.&#160;&#160;The perquisites assist in attracting and retaining the Named Executive Officers and, in the case of certain perquisites, promote health, safety and efficiency of our Named Executive Officers.&#160;&#160;These perquisites are as follows:</font></div><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="padding-top:12px;font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;"><tr><td style="width:48px;"></td><td></td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align:top"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;padding-left:24px;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#8226;</font></div></td><td style="vertical-align:top;"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Use of Company Leased Aircraft &#8211;</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#32;The Company permits employees, including the Named Executive Officers, to use Company aircraft for personal travel for themselves and their guests.&#160;&#160;Such travel generally is limited to a space available basis on flights that are otherwise business-related.&#160;&#160;Where a Named Executive Officer, or a guest of that officer, flies on a space available basis, the additional variable cost to the Company (such as fuel, catering, and landing fees) is de minimus.&#160;&#160;As a result, no amount is reflected in the Summary Compensation Table for that flight.&#160;&#160;Where the additional variable cost to the Company occurs on such a flight for solely personal purposes of that Named Executive Officer or guest, that cost is included in the Summary Compensation Table entry for that officer.&#160;&#160;Because it is rare for a flight to be purely personal in nature, fixed costs (such as hangar expenses, crew salaries and monthly leases) are not included in the Summary Compensation Table.&#160;&#160;In any case, in the event such a cost is non-deductible by the Company under the Internal Revenue Code, the value of that lost deduction is included in the Summary Compensation Table entry for that Named Executive Officer.&#160;&#160;When employees, including the Named Executive Officers, use Company aircraft for such travel they are attributed with taxable income in accordance with regulations pursuant to the Internal Revenue Code.&#160;&#160;The Company does not "gross up" or reimburse an employee for taxes he or she owes on such attributed income.&#160;&#160;The variable cost of the aircraft for personal travel, if any, is included in the respective entries in the Summary Compensation Table.&#160;&#160;See "Part III &#8211; Item 11 &#8211; Executive Compensation:&#160;&#160;Summary Compensation Table."</font></div></td></tr></table><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="padding-top:12px;font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;"><tr><td style="width:48px;"></td><td></td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align:top"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;padding-left:24px;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#8226;</font></div></td><td style="vertical-align:top;"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Enhanced Long Term Disability Benefit &#8211;</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#32;The Company provides the Named Executive Officers and other senior executive officers of the Company with an enhanced long term disability benefit.&#160;&#160;This benefit provides a supplemental replacement income benefit of 60% of average monthly compensation capped at $10,000 per month.&#160;&#160;The normal replacement income benefit applying to other of our employees is capped at $5,000 per month.</font></div></td></tr></table><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="padding-top:12px;font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;"><tr><td style="width:48px;"></td><td></td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align:top"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;padding-left:24px;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#8226;</font></div></td><td style="vertical-align:top;"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Enhanced Short Term Disability Benefit &#8211;</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#32;The Company provides the Named Executive Officers and other senior executive officers of the Company with an enhanced short term disability benefit.&#160;&#160;This benefit provides a supplemental replacement income benefit of 66&#160;2/3% of average monthly compensation, capped at $2,300 per week.&#160;&#160;The normal replacement income benefit applying to other of our employees is capped at $1,150 per week.</font></div></td></tr></table><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="padding-top:12px;font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;"><tr><td style="width:48px;"></td><td></td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align:top"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;padding-left:24px;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#8226;</font></div></td><td style="vertical-align:top;"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Miscellaneous &#8211;</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#32;Aside from benefits offered to its employees generally, the Company provided miscellaneous other benefits to its Named Executive Officers including the following (see "Part III &#8211; Item 11 &#8211; Executive Compensation:&#160;&#160;Summary Compensation Table &#8211; Components of 'All Other Compensation'"):</font></div></td></tr></table><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="padding-top:12px;font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;"><tr><td style="width:96px;"></td><td></td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align:top"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;padding-left:72px;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#9702;</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"></font></div></td><td style="vertical-align:top;"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Success Sharing &#8211; An incentive program offered to all of our employees that shares 15% of the excess earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, amortization and share based compensation expense over the highest previous year ("Success Sharing").</font></div></td></tr></table><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="padding-top:12px;font-family:Times New Roman; font-size:10pt;"><tr><td style="width:96px;"></td><td></td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align:top"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;padding-left:72px;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#9702;</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"></font></div></td><td style="vertical-align:top;"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Board Fees &#8211; Provided to Mr. Duncan as one of our directors. The Compensation Committee believes that it is appropriate to provide such board fees to Mr. Duncan given the additional oversight responsibilities and the accompanying liability incumbent upon members of our board.&#160;&#160;In determining the appropriate amount of overall compensation payable to Mr. Duncan in his capacity as&#160;&#160;Chief Executive Officer, the Compensation Committee does take into account any such board fees that are payable to Mr. Duncan.&#160;&#160;This monitoring of Mr. Duncan's overall compensation package for services rendered as Chief Executive Officer and as a director is done to ensure that Mr. Duncan is not being doubly compensated for the same services rendered to the Company.</font></div></td></tr></table><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;text-decoration:underline;">Retirement and Welfare Benefits</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#32;</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">&#8211;</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#32;</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">GCI 401(k) Plan.</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#160;&#160;In January 1987, we adopted an Employee Stock Purchase Plan (&#8220;GCI 401(k) Plan&#8221;) qualified under Section 401 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986. The GCI 401(k) Plan provides for acquisition of GCI&#8217;s Class A</font><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;">&#32;</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">common stock at market value as well as various mutual funds. We may match a percentage of the employees' contributions up to certain limits, decided by GCI&#8217;s Board of Directors each year. Named Executive Officers may, along with our employees generally, participate in our GCI 401(k) Plan in which we may provide matching contributions in accordance with the terms of the plan.</font></div><br><div style="text-align:center;"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">62</font></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"><div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:8pt;"><a style="font-family:Arial;font-size:8pt;" href="#sBA5EB157D68474C91E2F82221DA9B816"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:8pt;">Table of Contents</font></a></div></div><br><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">As of December 31, 2013, there remained 5,028,217 shares of Class A and 463,989 shares of Class B common stock allocated to our GCI 401(k) Plan and available for issuance by us or otherwise acquisition by the plan for the benefit of participants in the plan.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">&#8211; Deferred Compensation Plan and Arrangements.</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#160;&#160;The Company provides to certain of our employees, including our executive officers and Named Executive Officers, opportunities to defer certain compensation under our nonqualified, unfunded, deferred compensation plan ("Deferred Compensation Plan").&#160;&#160;In addition, we offer to our executive officers and to certain of our Named Executive Officers nonqualified, deferred compensation arrangements more specifically fashioned to the needs of the officer and us ("Deferred Compensation Arrangements").&#160;&#160;During 2013, none of our officers participated in the Deferred Compensation Plan.&#160;&#160;However, during 2013, two of our officers, both Named Executive Officers, participated in Deferred Compensation Arrangements specifically fashioned to their respective needs.&#160;&#160;These Deferred Compensation Arrangements enable these individuals to defer compensation in excess of limits that apply to qualified plans, like our GCI 401(k) Plan, and to pursue other income tax goals which they set for themselves.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Based upon its review of the Deferred Compensation Arrangements, our Compensation Committee concluded that the benefits provided under them are in each case both reasonable and an important tool in retaining the executive officers involved with those arrangements.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">&#8211; Welfare Benefits.</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#160;&#160;With the exception of the enhanced long term and short term disability benefits described previously, the Company provided to the Named Executive Officers the same health and welfare benefits provided generally to all other employees of the Company at the same general premium rates as charged to those employees.&#160;&#160;The cost of the health and welfare programs is subsidized by the Company for all eligible employees including the Named Executive Officers.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Performance Rewarded &#8211;</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Our Compensation Program is, in large part, designed to reward individual performance.&#160;&#160;What constitutes performance varies from officer to officer, depending upon the nature of the officer's responsibilities.&#160;&#160;Consistent with the Compensation Program, the Company identified key business metrics and established defined targets related to those metrics for each Named Executive Officer.&#160;&#160;In the case of each Named Executive Officer, the targets were regularly reviewed by management, from time to time, and provided an immediate and clear picture of performance and enabled management to respond quickly to both potential problems as well as potential opportunities.&#160;&#160;The Compensation Program also was used to establish and track corresponding applicable targets for individual management employees.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">In 2013, the Compensation Program was used in the development of each Named Executive Officer's individual performance goals and established incentive compensation targets.&#160;&#160;The Compensation Committee evaluated the performance of each of the executive officers and the financial performance of the Company and awarded incentive compensation as described above.&#160;&#160;See "Part III &#8211; Item 11 &#8211; Compensation Discussion and Analysis:&#160;&#160;Elements of Compensation &#8211; Incentive Compensation Plan."</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">In 2013, our Compensation Committee determined to increase the cash component of Mr. Chapados&#8217; base salary from $350,000 to $450,000 effective January 1, 2014.&#160;&#160;The increase to the cash component of Mr. Chapados&#8217; base salary was to compensate him for the additional responsibilities resulting from the increased growth of the Company.&#160;</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">In 2013, our Compensation Committee increased the cash component of Mr. Duncan's base salary from $840,000 to $925,000 effective January 1, 2014.  In addition, the Compensation Committee increased Mr. Duncan's annual Incentive Compensation target (exclusive of the former long-term incentive component) from $1,210,000 to $1,475,000 effective January 1, 2014. The increases to Mr. Duncan's base salary and Incentive Compensation are to compensate him for the additional responsibilities resulting from the growth of the Company.  The amounts disclosed under Part III &#8211; Item 11 &#8211; Short-Term Incentive Compensation &#8211; 2014 Incentive Compensation Targets reflect the increase to Mr. Duncan&#8217;s 2014 incentive compensation target.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">In 2013, our Compensation Committee modified the calculation of Mr. Duncan's Incentive Compensation target. Mr. Duncan's incentive compensation target will be calculated by multiplying the sum of his base salary, director cash </font></div><br><div style="text-align:center;"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">63</font></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"><div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:8pt;"><a style="font-family:Arial;font-size:8pt;" href="#sBA5EB157D68474C91E2F82221DA9B816"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:8pt;">Table of Contents</font></a></div></div><br><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">compensation, estimated value of stock grant for service as a director, and incentive compensation ("Total Compensation") by the percentage increase in Adjusted EBITDA from Adjusted EBITDA in 2013 and adding that to his target incentive compensation.  </font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Timing of Equity Awards &#8211;</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;text-decoration:underline;">Overview</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">.</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#160;&#160;Timing of equity awards under our Director Compensation Plan and equity awards under our Compensation Program varies with the plan or portion of that program.&#160;&#160;However, the Company does not, and has not in the past, timed its release of material nonpublic information for purposes of affecting the value of equity compensation.&#160;&#160;Timing issues and our grant policy are described further below.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;text-decoration:underline;">Director Compensation Plan</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">.</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#160;&#160;As a part of the Director Compensation Plan, we grant awards of our common stock to board members, including those persons who may also be serving as one or more of our executive officers.&#160;&#160;Mr. Duncan, a board member and Named Executive Officer, has been granted such awards in the past.&#160;&#160;These awards are made annually in June of each year in accordance with the terms of the Director Compensation Plan.&#160;&#160;The awards are made through our Stock Option Plan.&#160;&#160;See "Part III &#8211; Item 11 &#8211; Compensation Discussion and Analysis:&#160;&#160;Elements of Compensation &#8211; Stock Option Plan."</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;text-decoration:underline;">Incentive Compensation Plan</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">.</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#160;&#160;As a part of our Compensation Program, from time to time, we grant stock options and awards in our Class A common stock to our executive officers, including the Named Executive Officers.&#160;&#160;In particular, stock options and awards are granted in conjunction with the agreements that we enter into with Named Executive Officers pursuant to our Incentive Compensation Plan.&#160;&#160;The grants of such options and awards are typically made early in the year at the time our board finalizes the prior year incentive compensation plan payouts for each of the Named Executive Officers.&#160;&#160;All such options and awards are granted through the Stock Option Plan.&#160;&#160;See "Part III &#8211; Item 11 &#8211; Compensation Discussion and Analysis:&#160;&#160;Elements of Compensation &#8211; Incentive Compensation Plan" and "&#8211; Elements of Compensation &#8211; Stock Option Plan."</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;text-decoration:underline;">Stock Option Plan</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">.</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#160;&#160;As a part of our Compensation Program, from time to time, we grant stock options or awards in our Class A common stock to our executive officers, including the Named Executive Officers, and to certain of our advisors.&#160;&#160;In the case of an executive officer, these options or awards may be granted regardless of whether there is in place an agreement entered into with the officer under our Incentive Compensation Plan.&#160;&#160;In the case of a new hire and where we choose to grant options or awards, the grant may be done at the time of hire.&#160;&#160;Under the Stock Option Plan, the Compensation Committee may set the exercise price for our Class A common stock at not less than its fair market value.&#160;&#160;That value is presently determined on Nasdaq.&#160;&#160;In all cases, regardless of the identity of the grantee, the timing, amount and other terms of the grant of options or awards under our Stock Option Plan are determined in the sole discretion of our Compensation Committee.&#160;&#160;See "Part III &#8211; Item 11 &#8211; Compensation Discussion and Analysis:&#160;&#160;Elements of Compensation &#8211; Stock Option Plan."</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">In the event an executive level employee is hired or promoted during a year, that employee may be eligible to receive an award under the plans previously described in this section.&#160;&#160;Grants of awards in this context may be made at the recommendation of management and only with action of the Compensation Committee.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;text-decoration:underline;">Grant Policy</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">.</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#160;&#160;Under our grant policy, all approved grants are granted effective the date they were approved by the committee and are priced at the market value at the close of trading on that date.&#160;&#160;The terms of the award are then communicated immediately to the recipient.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Tax and Accounting Treatment of Executive Compensation &#8211;</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">In determining the amount and form of compensation granted to executive officers, including the Named Executive Officers, the Company takes into consideration both tax treatment and accounting treatment of the compensation.&#160;&#160;Tax and accounting treatment for various forms of compensation is subject to changes in, and changing interpretations of, applicable laws, regulations, rulings and other factors not within the Company's control.&#160;&#160;As a result, tax and accounting treatment is only one of several factors that the Company takes into account in designing the previously described elements of compensation.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><br><div style="text-align:center;"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">64</font></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"><div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:8pt;"><a style="font-family:Arial;font-size:8pt;" href="#sBA5EB157D68474C91E2F82221DA9B816"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:8pt;">Table of Contents</font></a></div></div><br><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Compensation Policies and Practices in Relation to Our Risk Management &#8211;</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">At the direction of our board, Company management has reviewed our compensation policies, plans and practices to determine whether they create incentives or encourage behavior that is reasonably likely to have a materially adverse effect on the Company.&#160;&#160;This effort included a review of our various employee compensation plans and practices as described elsewhere in this report.&#160;&#160;See "Part III &#8211; Item 11 &#8211; Compensation Discussion and Analysis: Process."</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">The purpose of the review was to evaluate risks and the internal controls we have implemented to manage those risks.&#160;&#160;The controls include multiple performance metrics, corporate-wide financial measures, statutory clawbacks on equity awards, and board and board committee oversight and approvals.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">In completing this review, our board and management believe risks created by our compensation policies, plans and practices that create incentives likely to have a material adverse effect on us are remote.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Shareholder Advisory Votes on Executive Compensation</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">The agenda for the 2014 annual meeting will include an opportunity for our shareholders to provide an advisory vote on executive compensation of our Named Executive Officers. The vote on executive compensation will be advisory only and is not binding on the Company or its board.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Our board views decisions as to compensation of Company named executive officers, including but not limited to those for 2013, as its responsibility.&#160;&#160;Our board takes this responsibility seriously and has gone to considerable effort to establish and implement a process for determining executive compensation as described elsewhere in this report.&#160;&#160;See "Part III &#8211; Item 11 &#8211; Compensation Discussion and Analysis."</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Our board carefully considers all proposals from our shareholders.&#160;&#160;However, in light of its responsibilities to the Company, our board may or may not follow the advice of those shareholder votes.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Our board contemplates next placing before our shareholders a proposal dealing with the frequency of shareholder advisory votes on executive compensation of our named executive officers during our 2017 annual shareholder meeting.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Executive Compensation</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Summary Compensation Table &#8211;</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">As of December 31, 2013, the Company did not have employment agreements with any of the Named Executive Officers.&#160;&#160;The following table summarizes total compensation paid or earned by each Named Executive Officer for fiscal years 2013, 2012 and 2011.&#160;&#160;The process followed by the Compensation Committee in establishing total compensation for each Named Executive Officer as set forth in the table is described elsewhere in this report.&#160;&#160;See "Part III &#8211; Item 11 &#8211; Compensation Discussion and Analysis."</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:center;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><br><div style="text-align:center;"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Potential Payments upon Termination or Change-in-Control</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">&#32;</font><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">&#8211;</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">As of December 31, 2013, there were no compensatory plans or arrangements providing for payments to any of the Named Executive Officers in conjunction with any termination of employment or other working relationship of such an officer with us (including without limitation, resignation, severance, retirement or constructive termination of employment of the officer).&#160;&#160;Furthermore, as of December 31, 2013, there were no such plans or arrangements providing for payments to any of the Named Executive Officers in conjunction with a change of control of us or a change in such an officer's responsibilities to us.&#160;&#160;However, all outstanding options and awards for each of our Named Executive Officers would vest upon his or her disability, retirement or death, or a change-in-control of the Company.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Nonqualified Deferred Compensation</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;font-weight:bold;">Deferred Compensation Plan &#8211;</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">We established our Deferred Compensation Plan in 1995 to provide a means by which certain of our employees may elect to defer receipt of designated percentages or amounts of their compensation and to provide a means for certain other deferrals of compensation.&#160;&#160;Employees eligible to participate in our Deferred Compensation Plan are determined by our board.&#160;&#160;We may, at our discretion, contribute matching deferrals in amounts as we select.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Participants immediately vest in all elective deferrals and all income and gain attributable to that participation.&#160;&#160;Matching contributions and all income and gain attributable to them vest on a case-by-case basis as determined by us.&#160;&#160;Participants may elect to be paid in either a single lump-sum payment or annual installments over a period not to exceed ten years.&#160;&#160;Vested balances are payable upon termination of employment, unforeseen </font></div><br><div style="text-align:center;"><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">70</font></div></div><hr style="page-break-after:always"><div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:8pt;"><a style="font-family:Arial;font-size:8pt;" href="#sBA5EB157D68474C91E2F82221DA9B816"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:8pt;">Table of Contents</font></a></div></div><br><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">emergencies, death or total disability of the participant or change of control of us or our insolvency.&#160;&#160;Participants become our general unsecured creditors with respect to deferred compensation benefits of our Deferred Compensation Plan.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Mr. Hughes' Deferred Compensation Arrangement with us consists of three components.&#160;&#160;The first component consisted of deferred compensation invested in 90,480 shares of Company Class A common stock.&#160;&#160;The second component is $1,212,359 accrued at year end of which $125,000 in salary were deferred and $110,214 of interest were accrued during 2013.&#160;&#160;This arrangement with us earns interest at the rate of 10% per year based upon the balance at the beginning of the year plus new salary deferrals during the year.&#160;&#160;The third component is $640,000 accrued at year end of which $30,000 were accrued for 2013 interest.&#160;&#160;This arrangement earns interest at 7.5% per year based upon the original $400,000 that was given to Mr. Hughes in consideration for his continued employment at the Company from January 1, 2006 through December 31, 2009.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:Arial;font-size:10pt;">Mr. Hughes' Deferred Compensation Arrangement provides that at his discretion or at termination of employment, he is entitled to receive the full amount owed in a lump sum, in monthly installments paid over a ten-year period, or in installments negotiated with the Company in accordance with statutory requirements.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;text-align:left;font-size:10pt;"><font Compensation Arrangement with us consists of deferred salary which earns interest on the amounts deferred at 9% per year.&#160;&#160;As of December&#160;31, 2013 and under this plan, there were accrued $729,309, of which $119,851 had accrued ($65,000 in principal plus $54,851 in interest) and $122,254 had been paid out during 2013.&#160;&#160;Additionally, effective January 1, 2007, the Company agreed to enter into a retention agreement with Mr. Lowber.&#160;&#160;In exchange for his commitment to remain in the employ of the Company through the end of 2010, the Company agreed to establish a deferred compensation account in the amount of $350,000 that vested on December 31, 2010.&#160;&#160;The account is to accrue interest at the rate of 7.25% per annum, compounding annually.&#160;&#160;The balance in that account was $501,763 as of December 31, 2013, of which $114,185 had accrued ($70,000 in principal plus $44,185 in interest) and $77,747 had been paid out during 2013.</font></div><div style="line-height:120%;font-size:10pt;"><font style="font-family:inherit;font-size:10pt;"><br></font></div><br><div style="text-align:center;"><div
